displayTitle: How a Coil Plants Currents in Solid Metal
kicker: ET LEVEL I // VARYING FIELDS & INDUCTION

beats.narration: Every eddy current test stands on three linked pieces of physics. Get these, and the whole method clicks into place.

payload.title: FIELDS & INDUCTION
payload.sub: Three laws turn a coil into a flaw finder.

beats.narration: First, Oersted: current makes a magnetic field circling the wire. Coil it, and the loops stack into one field set by ampere-turns, current times turns.
Two: change is everything. Direct current makes a steady field and induces nothing. Alternating current makes the field expand, collapse, and reverse every cycle, no motion required.

payload.heading: Two: only a changing field works

beats.narration: Three, Faraday: put a conductive part under that AC coil and induction is automatic. The voltage drives currents swirling in closed loops, eddy currents.

payload.heading: Coil and part act as a transformer

beats.narration: Lenz's law sets their direction: they oppose the change that made them. Two things are non-negotiable behind every test.

payload.sub: Both non-negotiable behind every eddy current test.
